basics.name: Michael Woodcock
basics.label: Researcher • Developer • Engineer
basics.summary: Interdisciplinary researcher and engineer integrating computing, psychology, and hardware design to build tools that advance research, learning, and human performance.

awards.title: Honorary Recognition for Departmental Service
awards.summary: Award created specifically in recognition of exceptional service to the Psychology Department.

awards.title: 1st Place Poster – PCEC Symposium
awards.summary: Low-Cost Centimeter-Level Robot Localization; received cash prize and top RocketJudge ratings.

awards.title: Outstanding Paper Presentation – SEPA
awards.summary: Awarded for research on mask-wearing and emotion recognition.

awards.title: Outstanding Student Scholar Award – GSPS
awards.summary: Awarded for CRAT mood/mindset research project.

work.name: Georgia Southern University
work.summary: Supported faculty across multiple departments through tutoring, technical troubleshooting, research assistance, and instructional material development.
highlights: Tutored students and assisted during hands-on labs
Reviewed submissions and created assignment manuals
Resolved technical issues with ITS
Supported research computing and lab operations

work.name: Sonic Motorsports
work.summary: Engineered custom parts and solutions for high-performance and vintage vehicles.
highlights: Restored and optimized Ferrari 250 GTO, Acura NSX, and other specialty vehicles
Performed advanced diagnostics on vehicles deemed irreparable by other shops
Designed and fabricated custom components

work.name: Honda Yamaha of Savannah
work.summary: Diagnosed and repaired mechanical and electrical issues on motorcycles and powersports equipment.
highlights: Performed intensive diagnostics on malfunctioning equipment
Managed inventory and improved service workflow
Provided diagnostic insights to manufacturers

work.name: Boaen Mechanical
work.summary: Managed project estimation, materials, and job progress for mechanical contracting projects.
highlights: Returned company to profitability through accurate cost estimation
Coordinated materials and supplies for job sites
Reported progress to senior management

research.name: SCIP Lab – Georgia Southern University
research.summary: Six-year research associate contributing to emotion recognition, mask occlusion, and test-format research.
highlights: Co-authored multiple peer-reviewed publications
Developed DANVA-2 PC and web-based testing tools
Created masked facial stimuli used in cross-cultural COVID-era studies
Recovered 20 years of lab data from a failing hard drive

research.name: Ratsketball Project
research.summary: Designed and built the 3D-printed operant chamber used in the Ratsketball study.
highlights: Constructed rat basketball court and automated scoring system
Co-authored publication on generative responding

research.name: CRAT Mood/Mindset Study
research.summary: Developed PsychoPy CRAT tasks enabling a year-long experimental study.
highlights: Supported 10 research assistants
Led to GSPS Outstanding Student Scholar Award

publications.name: Facial Occlusion with Medical Masks: Impacts on Emotion Recognition
Does Format Matter? Investigating the Impact of Test Format on Facial Emotion Recognition
Ratsketball: Using Low-Cost 3D Printed Operant Chambers to Probe for Generative Learning

projects.name: OCER-2 Career Exploration Platform
projects.summary: Full website built to replace a proposed wiki; now distributed nationally through STP.

projects.name: DANVA-2 PC & Web Tools
projects.summary: Standalone PC installer and Qualtrics-integrated web version for emotion recognition testing.

projects.name: Eye-Tracking Device
projects.summary: Low-cost eye-tracking hardware built for faculty research.
